title: News Trading with AI Bots – Can They Catch Moves Like Trump’s Tariff Pause?
author: dimuthlk
selftext:
Hey everyone, I’ve been diving into news trading lately and wondering how AI bots can play a role in catching those lightning-fast market moves like the craziness we saw today (April 9, 2025) with Trump’s 90-day tariff pause announcement (and the 125% hike on China). Markets went wild, and it got me thinking: can AI trading tools actually keep up with this kind of news-driven volatility?

I know some folks use bots to scrape headlines, social media (like X), and economic data to trigger trades, but how reliable are they for something as chaotic as a Trump tariff tweet? The speed of news hitting the wire or Truth Social in this case seems insane, and by the time traditional algos react, the move might already be priced in. Are there AI tools out there that can predict or at least ride these waves effectively?

For context, I saw reports that Wall Street flipped from panic to a surge in minutes after Trump’s post. Web sources like Reuters and Yahoo Finance noted the tariff pause sent stocks soaring, while the China escalation spooked others. Could an AI bot analyze sentiment fast enough to get ahead of that? Or is it still a human game of reflexes and luck?

Anyone here using AI trading setups for news events? What platforms or strategies do you lean on? I’m curious if there’s a way to automate catching these trades without getting burned by fakeouts or lag. Drop your thoughts, super interested to hear how you’re tackling this!
